hypothesis: If the CNN-GRU-attention architecture is extended into an inverse design framework using gradient-based optimization or generative adversarial approaches, then it will be possible to identify non-intuitive polymer sequences and surface spatial patterns that achieve target binding free energies through synergistic sequence-pattern complementarity rather than simple compositional matching, such that optimal adhesion at a given total strong-bead fraction is achieved by specific spatial correlations between polymer sequence motifs and surface patch geometries that cannot be predicted from composition alone—a hypothesis testable by generating inverse-designed sequence-pattern pairs, computing their binding free energies via umbrella sampling MD, and comparing the achieved ∆F values and structural specificity against composition-matched random controls.
